title: Star Wars video games
content:
{{Top|real|title=''Star Wars'' video games}}
[[File:Jedi_Academy_PC.png|thumb|right|200px|[[Dark Forces/Jedi Knight|''Jedi Knight'']] was a video-game series introducing [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyle Katarn]] and his apprentice [[Jaden Korr]].]]
A '''video game''' is any game played on a video-game console, any interactive game software, or a computer game where a video display is the primary feedback device.

==Game genres==
*[[Wikipedia:First-person shooter|'''First-person shooter''' ('''FPS''')]] games are video or computer games in which the player's viewpoint is a game character's perspective. They often feature guns and other combat weapons. ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' and its sequels are FPSs, as is ''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]''. See [[:Category:First-person shooters]] for more examples.

*'''[[Wikipedia:Space flight simulation game|Flight simulators]]''' are games that put the player in the position of piloting an air- or spacecraft against opponents or obstacles. The [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''X-wing'']] and [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''TIE Fighter'']] series, [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Rogue Squadron'']] series, [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Starfighter'']] series, and ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed]]'' are all flight simulators.

*[[Wikipedia:Real-time strategy|'''Real-time strategy''' ('''RTS''')]] games are usually computer games in which the player views multiple characters from a top-down point of view. In RTSs, players usually command multiple groups of characters without actually fighting themselves. Examples of RTSs include [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], ''[[Star Wars: Force Commander]]'', ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''.

*[[Wikipedia:Role-playing video game|'''Role-playing games''' ('''RPGs''')]] are video or computer games in which the player views the character from a third-person perspective and makes choices that ultimately change the games' story. ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' are RPGs. They are similar in some ways to tabletop [[roleplaying game]]s.

{{Anchor|Massively multiplayer online role-playing game}}
*[[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|'''Massively multiplayer online role-playing games''' ('''MMORPGs''')]] are online games where players take on characters and interact with each other on a broad scale. They often feature a variety of environments and numerous non-player characters (NPCs) to provide a more realistic feel. Examples include [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (video game)|''Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures'']] and ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.

==List of ''Star Wars'' games in order of publication==
===1980s===
====1982====
*'''''[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (1982 video game)|Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back]]''''' was the first ''[[Star Wars]]'' video game. Developed by [[Parker Brothers]] and released in [[1982]] for the [[Wikipedia:Atari 2600|Atari 2600]] and [[Wikipedia:Intellivision|Intellivision]] consoles, ''The Empire Strikes Back'' is an adaption of the [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|film of the same name]], though the game is effectively impossible to win: players take on the role of a Rebel pilot attempting to destroy [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], and the AT-ATs will eventually reach their goal.<ref name="Empire Strikes Back 1991">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (1982 video game)|''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back'' video game]]</ref>
====1983====
*'''''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Death Star Battle]]''''' was produced by Parker Brothers for the Atari 2600, [[Wikipedia:Atari 5200|Atari 5200]], and [[Wikipedia:Atari 8-bit|Atari 8-bit]] consoles that was released in [[1983]]. The game's objective is to pilot the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and destroy the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]].<ref name="Death Star Battle">''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Death Star Battle]]''</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: Jedi Arena]]''''' was produced solely for the Atari 2600 by Parker Brothers in 1983. ''Jedi Arena'' is a basic arena battle game that sees two [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] face off against each other in an arena, and players win by deflecting laser blasts from a remote at their opponent.<ref name="Jedi Arena">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Arena]]''</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: The Arcade Game]]''''' is an arcade game produced by [[Atari]] and released in [[July]] 1983 as a first-person space simulator based on the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the climax of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. The game was very popular and was re-released on a number of game consoles.<ref name="The Arcade Game">''[[Star Wars: The Arcade Game]]''</ref>

====1984====
*'''''[[Return of the Jedi (arcade game)|Star Wars: Return of the Jedi]]'''''
====1985====
*'''''[[The Empire Strikes Back (arcade game)|The Empire Strikes Back]]'''''
====1987====
*'''''[[Star Wars (1987 video game)|Star Wars]]''''' is a Japanese video game released in [[1987]] that adapts ''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope''. A side-scrolling platformer, the game was released for the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo Entertainment System|Famicom]] console, and takes liberties with the movie's storyline.<ref name="SW 1987">[[Star Wars (1987 video game)|''Star Wars'' 1987 video game]]</ref>

*'''''[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (1991 video game)|The Empire Strikes Back]]''''' was released for the Nintendo Entertainment System in December 1991. The player fights through the events of Episode V as [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="ESB 1991">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (1991 video game)|The Empire Strikes Back'' video game'']]</ref>

*'''''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]''''' was released on PlayStation 2 on [[February 13]] and is the sequel to ''Star Wars: Episode I Racer'', focusing on [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba's]] attempt to gain revenge on [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. ''Racer Revenge'' is a podracing game like ''Episode I Racer'', featuring thirteen tracks and twenty-two pilots, and was developed by [[Rainbow Studios]] and rated E.<ref name="Racer Revenge">''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]''</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''''' is the sequel to ''Star Wars: Starfighter'', and was released on [[March 10]] for the PlayStation 2 and [[May 13]] for the [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]]. ''Jedi Starfighter'' takes place prior to and during the events of ''[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Attack of the Clones]]'' and introduced a number of elements in the film prior to the movie's release.<ref name="Jedi Starfighter">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''''' is the sequel to ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', and was released by [[LucasArts]] and [[Raven Software]] on [[March 26]] on PC, Mac, Xbox, and GameCube. ''Jedi Outcast'' is set in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], seven years after ''Dark Forces II'', and centers on Kyle Katarn's battle against the [[Imperial Remnant]] and the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Reborn]].<ref name="Jedi Outcast">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''</ref>

*'''''[[LEGO Star Wars III: The Clone Wars]]'''''
*'''''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''''' is a massively multiplayer online role-playing game released by BioWare and LucasArts on December 20, 2011. The "sequel" to the two ''Knights of the Old Republic'' games, ''The Old Republic'' is set three hundred years after the original games, and succeeded ''Star Wars Galaxies'' as the primary ''Star Wars'' MMORPG. ''The Old Republic'' features eight classes divided into two factions and almost twenty planets, and the game was developed over the course of more than five years and also spawned an expansive multimedia metaseries.<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>

*'''''[[Angry Birds Star Wars]]''''' is an Angry Birds game released for mobile devices and computers on November 8. The game contains many ''Star Wars'' characters and 6 chapters based on the [[original trilogy]]. In 2013, the game was released on console along with the release of the second game. The series shut down in 2020.

==Canceled games==
*'''''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Ewok Adventure]]''''', for the Atari 2600, was developed alongside [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Death Star Battle|''Death Star Battle'']] but never released.<ref name="Hildebrandt">''[[Star Wars: The Art of the Brothers Hildebrandt]]''</ref>
*'''''[[Vernost]]''''', by LucasArts, would have centered on the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] battling for control of the volcanic planet [[Vernost (planet)|Vernost]].
*'''''[[The New Emperor]]'''''<ref name="Rogue Leaders">''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]''</ref>
*'''''[[Project Hermes]]'''''<ref name="Daggert">{{WebCite|url=http://www.daggert.net/Folio/Programming/Lucas/Mercury/Mercury1.htm|title=Project Hermes. Cancelled: May 2001|archivedate=20190720070921}}</ref>
*'''''[[Proteus]]''''' was the working title for an MMORPG that was developed alongside [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']] as a console-only game. Development began around 2003, but it was canceled after several months due to difficulties creating the interface.<ref name="Rogue Leaders" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Imperial Commando]]''''' was the planned sequel to ''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'' that was in planning even during the production of ''Republic Commando'', but the game was canceled in 2004 with only concept paintings made.<ref name="Rogue Leaders" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rebel Commando]]'''''<ref name="Cinelinx">{{WebCite|url=https://www.cinelinx.com/game-news/item/8965-star-wars-republic-commando-developer-talks-sequel-plans-and-sev-s-fate.html|title=Star Wars: Republic Commando Developer Talks Sequel Plans and Sev's Fate|author=[[Jordan Maison|Maison, Jordan]]|date=2016-02-10|work=[https://www.cinelinx.com Cinelinx.com]|archivedate=20160212083436}}</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic 3]]''''' was the planned third installment in the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' series. ''Knights of the Old Republic 3'' was canceled before it started official development, though the series received a "sequel" in the form of the spin-off MMORPG ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.
*'''''[[Star Wars: Smuggler]]''''' started development in 2004 but was canceled in 2005. The player would have assumed the role of a smuggler in an Imperial-controlled galaxy.<ref name="Rogue Leaders" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Battlefront III]]''''' was to be a sequel to the highly selling ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''.
*'''''[[Star Wars: Battlefront IV]]'''''<ref name="GameSpot">{{GameSpot|url=articles/former-lucasarts-employee-on-why-star-wars-battlefront-iii-failed/1100-6400936|text=Former LucasArts employee on why Star Wars: Battlefront III failed}}</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rogue Leaders: Rogue Squadron Wii]]'''''<ref name="IGNVideo">{{WebCite|url=http://ca.ign.com/articles/2014/10/13/cancelled-star-wars-rogue-squadron-wii-game-footage-found|title=Canceled Star Wars Rogue Squadron Wii Game Footage Found|author=Jose Otero|date=2014-10-14|work=[[Wikipedia:IGN|IGN]]|archivedate=20150725185349}}</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: X-Wing vs. Tie Fighter]]'''''<ref name="IGN">{{WebCite|url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2014/10/10/more-cancelled-star-wars-games-revealed|title=More Cancelled Star Wars Games Revealed|author=Moser, Cassidee|date=2014-10-10|work=IGN|archivedate=20200823201028}}</ref>
*[[Star Wars Trilogy (Wii)|'''''Star Wars Trilogy''''']]<ref name="IGN" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight III: Brink of Darkness]]'''''<ref name="Denofgeek">{{WebCite|url=http://www.denofgeek.com/us/movies/star-wars/241301/43-canceled-star-wars-projects/page/0/2|title=43 Cancelled Star Wars Projects|author=Leane, Rob|date=014-11-14|work=Den of Geek|archivedate=20180304015430}}</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed III]]'''''<ref name="Blackman">http://www.cinelinx.com/game-news/item/8536-haden-blackman-reveals-what-the-future-of-the-force-unleashed-could-have-been.html</ref>
*'''''[[Battle of the Sith Lords]]''''', code named '''Damage''', would have revived [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] in the [[Legacy era]] and had ties to ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''. The game was being developed by [[Red Fly Studio]]s, which was to be bought by LucasArts. In 2011, LucasArts opted not to buy the video-game company, and the project was subsequently canceled.
*'''''[[Star Wars: 1313]]''''' would have taken place in the deeper levels of Coruscant, specifically level 1313, and would have featured the darker side of ''Star Wars''. A Mature rating was expected. However, the game was never released, as [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] shut down LucasArts after acquiring [[Lucasfilm]].
*'''''[[Star Wars: First Assault]]''''' was a multiplayer ''Star Wars'' game in development at LucasArts. It was canceled along with ''Star Wars: 1313'' after the closure of LucasArts following Disney's Lucasfilm acquisition.
*[[Death Star (game)|'''''Death Star''''']]<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Outpost]]'''''<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rebel Agent]]'''''<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rebel Fury]]'''''<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rebel Jedi]]'''''<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rebel Scum]]'''''<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Rebel Warrior]]'''''<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''''[[Star Wars: Attack Squadrons]]''''' was an online multiplayer space combat game in development by [[Disney Interactive]] and [[Area 52 Games]]. The game was officially announced in 2013, with a five-month beta occurring in 2014 before cancellation. The game would have featured modifiable ships, 16 player matches, and three battle modes.
*'''''[[Star Wars: Dark Squadron]]'''''<ref name="Nerdist">{{WebCite|url=https://nerdist.com/2-previously-unknown-star-wars-video-games-surface|title=2 Previously Unknown STAR WARS Video Games Surface|author=Marnell, Blair|date=2016-08-16|work=[[Wikipedia:Nerdist Industries|Nerdist]]|archivedate=20171229142101}}</ref>
*'''''[[Project Ragtag]]''''' was in development by the studio [[Wikipedia:EA Vancouver|EA Vancouver]] after [[Visceral Games]], the game's previous developers, were closed down. It was canceled in early [[2019]].
*'''''[[Star Wars: Galactic Conflict]]'''''<ref name="GPlay">{{GooglePlay|url=apps/details?id=com.gamesword.twsw&hl=en&gl=US|text=星球大戰：銀河衝突}}</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars Episode VII: Shadows of the Sith]]''''' was to focus on [[Ben Skywalker]]. It never progressed beyond an initial pitch.
*[[Shadow of the Sith (video game)|'''''Shadow of the Sith''''']] would have focused on [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] in a futuristic time period.
*'''''[[Star Wars Scene Maker: Rebels]]''''' would have let players place various characters in various scenes from the ''Star Wars Rebels'' animated series.
*'''[[Untitled Chewbacca game]]'''<ref name="Nerdist" />
*'''[[Untitled fighting game]]'''<ref name="Denofgeek" />
*'''[[Untitled Electronic Arts first-person shooter game]]'''<ref name="EA FPS">{{SW|url=news/ea-lucasfilm-games|text=Electronic Arts and Lucasfilm Games Announce New ''Star Wars'' Titles in Development from Respawn Entertainment}}</ref>
*'''''[[Star Wars: Project Hestia]]'''''<ref name="Project Hestia">{{ArtStation|subdomain=choochtoons|username=choochtoons|url=projects/rJPdJ5|name=[[Chuck Suong]]|text=STAR WARS ~ "Hestia" : never published.|date=2023-08-02|archivefile=File:ChuckSuong-ProjectHestiaDemo-ArtStation.png}}</ref>
